data.title: Binance USDT spot briefly rose to $1.02, but has now fallen back to $1.0016
data.description: Market data shows that USDT briefly rose to $1.02, possibly due to market fluctuations, and has now fallen back to $1.0016.

data.title: Chainlink’s non-circulating supply wallet unlocks 18.75 million LINK and deposits them on Binance
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, Chainlink's non-circulating supply wallet was unlocked again after 4 months and 18.75 million LINK (about US$3.87 million) was deposited into Binance.

data.title: Zerobase founder: Immediately add funds to the liquidity pool and users can withdraw them instantly
data.description: Zerobase founder Mirror Tang posted on the X platform, "Users who need to withdraw funds quickly can do so, and the pool will be immediately replenished."

data.title: Whale '7 Siblings' Acquires ETH Amid Market Decline
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on-chain data analyst Yu Jin has observed that the whale or institution known as '7 Siblings' has resumed purchasing Ethereum (ETH) following a significant market drop. The entity borrowed 40 million USDC from Aave and has already spent 5 million USDC to acquire 1,326 ETH at a price of $3,771 within the past hour.

This whale or institution has a history of buying ETH during market downturns and has profited considerably from these transactions. On August 5th of last year, when ETH fell by 15%, '7 Siblings' purchased 100,000 ETH at approximately $2,270 each. Similarly, on April 7th of this year, they acquired 25,000 ETH at around $1,700 each following a 10% decline in ETH's value.

data.title: Aster: Completed the repurchase of 100 million ASTER tokens
data.description: Aster officially announced on the X platform that it has completed a buyback round of 100 million ASTER tokens. All purchased tokens are stored at the following address: 0x02DCd5b2DdE0F6edb4B797DA468fBc52F23f49Dc.

data.title: Yi Lihua: Don’t buy the dip yet, wait patiently for the situation to become clear
data.description: Yi Lihua posted on X that he has not yet bought the bottom and will wait patiently for the situation to become clear. The decline is far greater than expected.

data.title: The Proliferation of Privacy on Ethereum
data.description:
The Kohaku project is kicking off Ethereum's "privacy by default" era.
source: https://www.bankless.com/read/proliferation-of-privacy-on-ethereum

data.title: Binance issued an announcement regarding the depegging of USDE, BNSOL, and BETH, and is conducting a comprehensive investigation into affected users and details.
data.description: Binance has officially released an announcement providing users with an update on the recent price decoupling issues involving USDE, BNSOL, and WBETH tokens. This incident resulted in forced liquidation of some users' positions. Our team is currently conducting a comprehensive review of affected users, the liquidation details, and the subsequent compensation plan. We are also strengthening our risk management mechanisms to reduce the likelihood of similar incidents recurring.

data.title: Market Enters New Phase Amid Structural Damage, Says Trader
data.description: According to BlockBeats, trader Eugene Ng Ah Sio stated on his personal channel that the market has experienced structural damage today. He emphasized that the market is now entering the next phase of its cycle, urging caution moving forward.

data.title: Aster Completes Token Buyback Ahead of Market Decline
data.description: According to PANews, Aster announced early on October 11 that it has completed a buyback of 100 million ASTER tokens prior to the recent market downturn. The company stated that all purchased tokens have been stored in a publicly accessible blockchain address.

data.title: Yi Lihua: There is no insider information in the liquidation, it is just because the investment research data shows that the market will fall.
data.description: Yi Lihua posted on X: "We are openly liquidating our positions and have repeatedly reminded everyone that we are transparent on-chain and have even detected certain behaviors. This is the first time since we bought the dip at around 1,000, because investment research data indicates a fall. There is no insider information, only data. We did not liquidate at the peak. The OGs in the cryptocurrency circle compete on who can survive longer, not on who can earn every penny."

data.title: Crypto KOL: The market crash may be due to the combined impact of USDe revolving loans, doubling of margin leverage, and Trump's trade war, causing heavy losses for market makers
data.description: Crypto influencer Hanba Longwang posted on the X platform that the market crash may be due to the 12% USDe subsidy, which led many market users to engage in USDe revolving loans. Due to the impact of Trump's trade war, USDe was hit by a premium, leading to the liquidation of USDe revolving loans and further declines in USDe. Furthermore, some whales and market makers used USDe as margin in contracts. Due to the USDe depegging discount, their leverage was inexplicably doubled, ultimately causing even a 1x long position to be liquidated. This further triggered a chain reaction, causing the prices of small copycat contracts to plummet, and USDe to plummet or even double, ultimately causing heavy losses for market makers.

data.title: Tether Issues Additional 1 Billion USDT on Ethereum
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Onchain Lens has reported that Tether recently minted an additional 1 billion USDT on the Ethereum blockchain. This issuance occurred approximately three hours ago, expanding the supply of the stablecoin on the network.

data.title: A whale made a profit of $21.82 million by closing its short position at a low point. It had previously liquidated its ETH position for a profit of $11.6 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, a whale closed out its short positions at the lowest point of the sharp market decline this morning. Its BTC closing price was $106,216 and its ETH closing price was $3,433, generating a profit of approximately $21.82 million. This whale, having previously liquidated its ETH position at $4,221 on September 22nd for a profit of $11.6 million, shorted $137 million worth of BTC and ETH on Hyperliquid, opening at $120,892 and $4,502, respectively.

data.title: Crypto.com CEO and Wintermute team members responded to market FUD: The institution is operating normally, and the rumors of bankruptcy are false.
data.description: In response to market rumors that "Crypto.com and Wintermute are suspected of collapsing," Crypto.com CEO Kris Marzsalek commented that "the system is running perfectly and it is unfounded FUD." Wintermute team BD and partner Arnaud said the statement was a rumor.

data.title: Bitcoin OG has closed most of its short positions, making a profit of over $160 million in 30 hours
data.description: According to LookonChain, a Bitcoin OG whale that previously shorted BTC and ETH has closed most of its short positions, making a profit of over $160 million in 30 hours. It currently holds a short position of 821.6 BTC (worth $92 million).

data.title: Bitcoin Investor Profits Amid Market Downturn
data.description: According to Foresight News, Onchain Lens monitoring has revealed that a prominent Bitcoin investor has closed their Ethereum short position, which was leveraged 12 times, amid a significant market decline. This move resulted in a profit of $72.33 million. Additionally, the investor closed most of their Bitcoin short position, leveraged 10 times, earning $81 million. Currently, they still hold an unrealized profit of $7 million.

data.title: Analysis: The actual market liquidation amount may exceed $40 billion, with HyperLiquid liquidation amount alone reaching nearly $7 billion
data.description: On-chain data analyst MLM Yu X posted a post stating that the liquidation figures circulating in the market are incorrect and that the actual number is likely much higher, ranging from $30 billion to $40 billion or even higher. On Hyperliquid alone, nearly $7 billion was liquidated.

data.title: Primitive Ventures founder: The market crash may be caused by a large-scale liquidation of an institution on Binance, and the USDe price difference reflects the differences between platforms
data.description: Primitive Ventures founder Dovey posted on the X platform, speculating that the recent market crash was caused by a large-scale margin call on Binance by a major institution (possibly a cross-margin trading firm). While further analysis is needed, preliminary data indicates that the price of USDe on Binance plummeted to as low as $0.6, while prices on other exchanges remained relatively strong. Furthermore, there has been a significant discrepancy in trading volume between tokens listed on Binance and those not listed on Binance.

data.title: Reeves presses CEOs to promote UK optimism before tax-hike budget
data.description:
Chancellor Rachel Reeves has called on UK business leaders to promote confidence in Britain’s economy.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/reeves-presses-ceos-to-promote-uk-optimism/
